key_insight: Geometrically necessary boundaries (GNBs) in cold-rolled Fe-3%Si act as the main carriers of long-range residual elastic strain, while incidental dislocation boundary (IDB) cells form inside GNB-delimited regions with similar strain levels.
engineering_relevance: This clarifies how deformation substructures store and distribute residual strain in electrical steels, informing recovery, recrystallization, and microstructure-aware processing models.
method: Dark-field X-ray microscopy (DFXM) for simultaneous 3D, bulk-sensitive mapping
Correlated intragranular misorientation with residual elastic strain in a 50% cold-rolled grain
Segmented GNB and IDB substructures to compare strain partitioning
evidence_signal: The study reports direct 3D experimental measurements showing that GNBs separate subdomains with distinct mean d-spacing and accommodate nearly all long-range residual elastic strain.
watch_item: Results are demonstrated on a single grain in one deformed alloy state, so broader generalization across processing conditions and compositions should be validated.
paper_title: Geometrically necessary boundaries accommodate the residual elastic strain in cold-rolled Fe-3%Si
